qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Safe MdCtx digest_final API permits adjacent memory overwrite
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function and missing output-length guard as the advisory and upstream fix: `MdCtxRef::digest_final()` in `openssl/src/md_ctx.rs` forwards the caller buffer to `EVP_DigestFinal()` without rejecting undersized output slices.

selected_finding.title: Safe MdCtx digest_final API permits adjacent memory overwrite

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory exactly: both identify `openssl::md_ctx::MdCtxRef::digest_final()` as missing a caller-buffer length check before `EVP_DigestFinal()`, and the copied PoC demonstrated the resulting out-of-bounds write into adjacent memory.
evidence: The advisory states that `MdCtxRef::digest_final()` always writes `EVP_MD_CTX_size(ctx)` bytes to `out` and overflows undersized caller buffers; the upstream fix in commit `826c3888b77add418b394770e2b2e3a72d9f92fe` adds `if self.size() > len as usize { return Err(ErrorStack::get()); }` to `openssl/src/md_ctx.rs` before `EVP_DigestFinal()`.
The final finding report describes the same source-to-sink path: safe Rust caller -> `MdCtxRef::digest_final(&mut [u8; 1])` -> OpenSSL writes the full SHA-512 digest -> adjacent memory overwrite.

The copied PoC run at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_d1b85c9e/.judge/poc_runs/mdctx_digest_final_overflow/run.log` logged `written=64`, `first_corruption_index=Some(0)`, and `EVOHUNT_PROOF canary_corrupted=true written=64`, which is direct runtime evidence that the advisory-described overwrite occurred.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC called `MdCtxRef::digest_final()` with SHA-512 and a 1-byte output buffer, returned `written=64`, observed the first adjacent canary byte change at index 0, and emitted `EVOHUNT_PROOF canary_corrupted=true written=64`.
